workOrder.select: maintenance_record_id, name, work_order_status, maintenance_type, asset_id, is_customer_billable, billing_account_id, invoice_id, invoice_status, cost_line_count, internal_subtotal, sell_subtotal, tax_total, sell_total, created_at, last_updated_at

costLines.select: id, line_type, description, quantity, unit_cost, sell_amount, cost_total, sell_line_total, is_taxable, tax_rate, tax_amount, notes, created_at

serviceHistory.select: asset_id, service_record_id, service_record_type, service_name, service_type, outcome, status, opened_at, completed_at, cost_summary, downtime_minutes, service_sort_at

props.children: Prior faults and structured prompts assembled from asset service history. The technician confirms all findings — the system never auto-closes or auto-certifies a repair.
Recent failed inspections and open maintenance records for this asset. Review before documenting to avoid repeating prior diagnosis steps.
No recent faults found in asset service history. Complete the documentation prompts based on direct inspection.
Complete all required fields (*) in one pass. Escalation reason is required when the work order is escalated.
Describe the symptom or fault as found. Include sensory observations (noise, leak, error code). Do not infer a cause.
Describe the work performed. What was replaced, adjusted, or cleaned? Note any deviations from OEM procedure.
Total technician hours charged to this work order.
List part numbers and descriptions of components consumed. Leave blank if no parts were used.
List any outstanding parts required to complete the repair. Informs your status selection below — choose Waiting for Parts if parts are still needed.
Additional observations about equipment condition, deferred items, or recommended follow-up.
Select a reason if this repair exceeds branch skill, time, or authorization limits. Escalation is a first-class outcome — use it instead of leaving notes in free text.
Exceeds branch skill — requires specialist knowledge or certification not available at this branch.
Exceeds authorization limit — repair cost or scope requires manager or regional approval.
Exceeds time available — work order must be transferred or deferred due to scheduling constraints.
Parts unavailable — work order on hold pending parts procurement.
Safety concern — management review required before returning unit to service.
OEM / specialist required — manufacturer or third-party intervention required.
Recent maintenance and inspection records for this asset — most recent first.
No service history found for this asset.
This work order is billable and completed. Generate a draft invoice to proceed with customer billing.
Internal work order — costing is recorded for fleet analytics only. No customer invoice will be generated.

props.label: Fault description
props.name: repair_fault_description
props.placeholder: Describe the symptom or fault as found. Include sensory observations (noise, leak, error code). Do not infer a cause.

props.label: Repair action taken
props.name: repair_action
props.placeholder: Describe the work performed. What was replaced, adjusted, or cleaned? Note any deviations from OEM procedure.

props.label: Parts used
props.name: repair_parts_used
props.placeholder: List part numbers and descriptions of components consumed. Leave blank if no parts were used.

props.label: Parts still needed
props.name: repair_parts_needed
props.placeholder: List any outstanding parts required to complete the repair. Informs your status selection below — choose Waiting for Parts if parts are still needed.

props.label: Technician findings
props.name: repair_technician_findings
props.placeholder: Additional observations about equipment condition, deferred items, or recommended follow-up.

options.label: In Progress — repair started but not complete
Waiting for Parts — parts ordered or needed
Escalated — requires further authorization or skill

then.value: Fault description, repair action, labor hours, and outcome status are required.

data.p_data: {{mergeEntityData(data.maintenanceRecordCurrentState.entity_versions[0].data, 'status', state.repair_status_value, 'fault_description', state.repair_fault_description, 'repair_action', state.repair_action, 'labor_hours', state.repair_labor_hours, 'parts_used', state.repair_parts_used, 'parts_needed', state.repair_parts_needed, 'technician_findings', state.repair_technician_findings)}}

onError.value: Failed to save repair documentation. Please try again.

props.label: Escalation notes
props.name: escalation_notes
props.placeholder: Describe the specific condition requiring escalation. Include any attempts already made and the observed outcome.

onError.value: Failed to escalate work order. Please try again.
